items.title: Pahang police to record more statements in probe into student’s claim of being slapped by politician
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 3 — Pahang police will record the statements of several witnesses to complete investigations of a public university student who claimed he was slapped by a politician in Janda Baik, Bentong on May 20. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the student, 23, lodged a police report at 12.11 pm last Saturday (May 30) about the alleged slapping incident that occurred on May 20 during a university programme. “The police have taken the statements of both the suspect and the victim and will record statements from several other witnesses to complete the investigation,” he said.

items.title: Carrying excess Zamzam water could land haj pilgrims in trouble
items.summary: MAKKAH: Haj pilgrims have been warned against carrying excessive amounts of Zamzam water home, as doing so could result in their luggage being detained or inspected by Saudi authorities. Haj Pilgrims Operations deputy director Mohd Ridzuan Yusoff said that despite repeated reminders each year, some pilgrims still attempted to conceal extra Zamzam water in their luggage. He said the practice violated airline regulations and Saudi rules, and could lead to luggage being held for inspection.

items.title: Guan Eng: Banks should do CSR with one-year interest pause for MSMEs' existing loans; interest-free RM50k new loans
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Malaysia’s banking industry should “step up and do its Corporate Social Responsibility” (CSR) by urgently helping out local micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) with loan relief measures, DAP’s Bagan MP Lim Guan Eng said today. Noting that banks’ before-tax profits grew from RM48.3 billion in 2024 to RM54.8 billion in 2025, Lim said the banks’ 13.5 per cent or RM6.5 billion growth in before-tax profits could help “offset” the expected contraction in growth for Malaysia’s manufacturing industry. Lim said the government must urgently use fiscal and monetary measures to help Malaysia’s 1.1 million MSMEs reduce costs and generate businesses, to counter the negative effects of an expected economic slowdown.

items.title: Retiree loses nearly RM100,000 pension savings to TikTok investment scam
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A retired civil servant lost nearly RM100,000 in pension savings after falling victim to a non-existent investment scheme. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said the 59-year-old lodged a report at the Kuala Terengganu police station at 8.24pm yesterday. He said the victim was attracted by an investment advertisement on TikTok on May 14 before contacting the syndicate via Telegram and making several transactions to participate in the scheme.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia opens higher on bargain hunting, Wall Street gains
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia opened higher on Wednesday as investors engaged in bargain hunting following the extended market closure last week, while positive sentiment from Wall Street's overnight gains also supported buying interest. At 9.33am, the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI rose 5.34 points, or 0.31 per cent, to 1,688.41 from Friday's close of 1,683.07. The index opened 4.06 points higher at 1,687.13.

items.title: Man drowns during family outing near Batang Ai Dam
items.summary: KUCHING: A family outing during the Gawai Dayak celebrations and school holidays ended in tragedy when a man drowned in the Batang Ai Dam area in Lubok Antu yesterday. A Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department spokesman said the body of Rudie Lan, 31, was recovered at 9.41pm by the Water Rescue Team at a depth of about 5m, within a one-metre radius of where he was last seen. The spokesman said the victim's family sought assistance from the department and other agencies after their own search efforts proved unsuccessful.

items.title: Insurance outlook: Bumps over short term but long-term growth intact
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's insurance sector is facing near-term pressure from slower premium growth and rising claims costs. But analysts at MBSB Research remain positive on the sector's longer-term prospects, supported by healthy dividend yields, improving life insurance momentum and ongoing efforts to tackle medical inflation. The firm said the first key headwind is slower premium growth, particularly for general insurance.

items.title: Six Tanjung Karang shophouses gutted in blaze
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations division assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said firefighters received an emergency call at 11.27pm and arrived at the scene, located in front of a petrol station, four minutes later. He said the blaze involved six single-storey Class A shophouses. "The fire affected about 90 per cent of all six premises.

items.title: Prioritise young, new faces for Johor polls to win youth vote – Azalina
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Barisan Nasional (BN) component parties should consider fielding more young and fresh faces in the upcoming Johor state election, particularly in constituencies with a large youth voter base, says Umno information chief Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said. She said the move would not only benefit BN but also help nurture a new generation of leaders who could eventually form the party's future leadership through the state assembly. Commenting on the dissolution of the Johor state assembly, Azalina said the state government, led by Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, was entitled to seek dissolution with the consent of the Johor Palace.

items.title: Gold eases on stronger oil as fresh Mideast hostilities erupt
items.summary: Spot gold fell 0.2 per cent to US$4,476.50 per ounce by 0103 GMT. US gold futures for August delivery fell 0.3 per cent to US$4,504.40. Spot silver fell 0.5 per cent to US$74.73 per ounce, platinum lost 0.2 per cent to US$1,932.25, and palladium fell 0.3 per cent to US$1,365.25.

items.title: Keiko Fujimori: Peru's biggest political loser eyes election win
items.summary: LIMA: Peru's Keiko Fujimori is a perennial political loser. But on this, her fourth attempt, the daughter of disgraced late president Alberto Fujimori may have her best chance at power. DYNASTY Alberto Fujimori led Peru through the turbulent 1990s, crushing Maoist Shining Path rebels and taming hyperinflation.

items.title: China's real estate funk drags down yet another sector
items.summary: BEIJING: Providers of property services in China are struggling to collect management fees from disgruntled homeowners, threatening their revenue and house prices in general, while making them the latest victim of the country's prolonged real estate slump. There, distressed property developers owe the service fees for unsold flats. Onewo, which uses the brand Vanke Service, is a property management unit of state-backed developer China Vanke.

items.title: Johor election: Onn Hafiz advises caution after fake candidate list appears
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: State Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has advised the people to be cautious of slander and lies after a list of coalition candidates for the upcoming Johor election began circulating. He said the list, which has been spread via social media and WhatsApp since yesterday was fake. He said any announcement regarding BN candidates would be made only through official party channels.

items.title: Beyond bricks and mortar, Nga Kor Ming says arts and culture are key to thriving cities
items.summary: IPOH, June 3 — A city cannot thrive on physical infrastructure alone as arts, culture and human connection are equally important in creating communities with a sense of belonging and identity, says Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming. “A city cannot thrive on physical infrastructure alone. Without arts, culture and human connection, even the most modern city risks losing its soul.

items.title: US job openings rise by the most since 2021; hiring weak amid economic uncertainty
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US job openings increased by the most in five years in April, but the surge likely overstates the labour market's health, as hiring declined against the backdrop of economic uncertainty stemming from the Iran war. The Job Openings and Labour Turnover Survey, or JOLTS report, from the Labour Department on Tuesday also showed resignations dropped to the lowest level in nearly six years in April, a sign of lack of confidence in the job market. The professional and business services industry accounted for roughly 91 per cent of the jump in job openings in April.

items.title: The end of one-way messaging: What new data reveals about the future of customer engagement
items.summary: CUSTOMER engagement has already moved beyond one-way messaging. Infobip's latest Messaging Trends Report shows that interactions continue to grow across messaging apps, email and voice, with hundreds of billions of engagements taking place each year. Messaging platforms, customer data and automation systems are often deployed independently.

items.title: Japan's govt finalises US$19bil extra budget to subsidise surging fuel costs
items.summary: TOKYO: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's government on Wednesday finalised a US$19 billion supplementary budget for this fiscal year to cushion the impact of rising energy costs on households amid the risk of a prolonged Middle East crisis. The 3.1 trillion yen (US$19.47 billion) extra budget will be funded entirely through deficit-financing bonds, with the government seeking to avoid increasing overall bond issuance to the market by offsetting it with stronger tax revenues and non-tax income. The additional spending will primarily replenish contingency reserves used to subsidise gasoline costs and utility bills as tensions in the Middle East continue.

items.title: Meta scales back plan for internal mouse-tracking tech, citing staff concerns
items.summary: CALIFORNIA: Meta is dialling back elements of its plan to collect employee mouse movements, keystrokes and other actions for use as AI training data, it said in an internal memo on Tuesday, following weeks of angry pushback from staffers. New controls will allow employees to pause the data collection for up to 30 minutes at a time and request exemptions from the initiative, according to the memo, authored by Stephane Kasriel, a vice president in Meta's AI model-building Superintelligence Labs unit. A Meta spokesperson declined to comment.

items.title: Microsoft reveals new quantum chip made with AI, says it will have systems by 2029
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO: Microsoft on Tuesday unveiled a new quantum computing chip that it redesigned with the help of AI, saying it now believes it will have commercially useful quantum machines by 2029. The new target date puts Microsoft on track to have quantum computers the same year as rival IBM, which last month said it plans to spend $10 billion on quantum machines. On Tuesday, Microsoft unveiled a new chip called Majorana 2, a follow-on from its first Majorana chip last year.

items.title: Sarawak longhouses among world’s most authentic cultural tourism products, says Tiong
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Sarawak longhouses have unique advantages that make them ideal community-based tourism products, said Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing. He said in line with the ministry’s Malaysia Homestay Experience Programme, longhouses offer a cultural experience not found anywhere else in the world. He added that, in addition to preserving cultural heritage, developing community tourism could create economic opportunities for local residents through homestays, handicrafts, traditional food, cultural performances, and other activities.

items.title: Scientists find yeast in ancient Iceman's guts — and make bread
items.summary: PARIS: Yeast has been growing in the guts of a frozen mummy called Oetzi the Iceman for thousands of years, scientists have discovered, telling AFP they used it to make sourdough bread. This has allowed scientists to carefully study Oetzi, who offers an incredibly rare window into ancient human life. Sarhan said.

items.title: Johor cops detain woman over newborn’s death after body found at housing block
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 3 — Police have arrested a local woman for allegedly throwing her newborn baby girl from the 10th floor of a flat near here in an incident on May 2. Johor acting police chief DCP Hoo Chuan Huat said police received a report regarding the discovery of the baby’s body at the flat’s parking area at about 2.40 pm. He said the 22-year-old suspect was arrested at noon today in the compound of Sultanah Aminah Hospital (HSA).

items.title: 14-year-old Alor Star girl missing since Monday
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Police are seeking public help in locating a 14-year-old girl who has been missing since Monday. In a Facebook post, Kota Setar police said Nurul Munirah Mohd Shahril stands about 150cm and weighs about 68kg. Anyone with information on her whereabouts should contact Sergeant Syahidah at 013-9630772, the Kota Setar police headquarters hotline at 04-7747222 or the Kedah police headquarters hotline at 04-7741222.

items.title: Penang police bust three international scam syndicates, arrest 32 in Bukit Mertajam raids
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, June 3 – Penang police busted three international scam syndicates and arrested 32 individuals, including four local men, during three separate raids on premises used as call centers around Bukit Mertajam yesterday. Penang Police Chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the operation, conducted by the Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) of the Penang Regional Police Headquarters (IPK), also led to the seizure of various equipment used by the syndicates, valued at an estimated RM123,000. Azizee said the second raid uncovered an USDT cryptocurrency exchange scam syndicate targeting victims from Taiwan and led to the arrest of 15 individuals comprising six men and a woman from Taiwan, two men and two women from Vietnam, and four local men aged between 20 and 49.

items.title: Johor BN says viral candidate list is fabricated, urges members not to be misled
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 3 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) has dismissed as fake a purported list of the coalition’s candidates for the upcoming state election that has been circulating on social media since this afternoon. Johor BN secretary Datuk Abdul Halim Suleiman said the list was fabricated by irresponsible parties to fuel speculation and confuse party members. A list purportedly containing BN’s candidates for all 56 state constituencies went viral on social media today, including the name of Machap assemblyman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.

items.title: SpaceX targets US$1.75tril valuation in all-primary IPO next week
items.summary: NEW YORK: SpaceX, Elon Musk's rocket and satellite company, plans to target a valuation of US$1.75 trillion in its blockbuster initial public offering (IPO), which will consist entirely of new shares, three people familiar with the matter told Reuters on Tuesday. The IPO is expected to be structured as an all-primary offering, meaning all proceeds would go to the company and existing SpaceX shareholders will not be able to sell any of their shares in the IPO, the sources said. Reuters previously reported the company was considering a preliminary valuation of around US$1.75 trillion.

items.title: US govt appeals judge's order for tariff refunds
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The Trump administration on Tuesday formally appealed a judge's order for refunds of the US president's global tariffs after they were struck down by the Supreme Court earlier this year. At stake is some US$166 billion in revenue. A refunds system handled by US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) has already begun to process repayments.

items.title: Johor BN dismisses viral candidate list as fake
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) has dismissed as fake a purported list of candidates for the upcoming state election that has been circulating on social media since yesterday afternoon. Johor BN secretary Datuk Abdul Halim Suleiman said the list was fabricated by irresponsible parties to fuel speculation and mislead party members. A list purportedly naming BN candidates for all 56 state constituencies went viral on social media today, including Machap assemblyman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.

items.title: Ringgit starts week softer against US dollar after holiday break
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened slightly lower against the US dollar on Wednesday as better-than-expected United States (US) economic data and uncertainty in Middle East kept the greenback ticking higher. At 8am, the local currency slipped to 3.9680/9725 against the greenback from last Friday's close of 3.9625/9670. "The US Dollar Index remained well supported as business sentiment improved in the US, with the manufacturing sector index rising to 54.0 points in May from 52.7 points in April, while job openings stood at 7.62 million in April, exceeding consensus estimates of 6.86 million.

items.title: Pahang police Logistics Department chief killed in crash in Hulu Selangor
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A senior police officer was killed when the motorcycle he was riding collided with a lorry in Jalan Batang Kali-Genting, Hulu Selangor, yesterday. Deputy Hulu Selangor police chief Deputy Superintendent Mohd Asri Mohd Yunus said Pahang Logistics Department chief Superintendent Yuslimezam Hashim, 51, died at the scene. Asri said Yuslimezam's body was taken to the Kuala Kubu Baru Hospital for post-mortem examination.

items.title: High oil prices could shift consumer behavior in 2H – Goldman CEO
items.summary: NEW YORK: Goldman Sachs chief executive officer David Solomon expects consumer behaviour to change in the second half of 2026 if inflation picks up, fuelled by higher oil prices. "You're going to see more shifts in consumer behavior," Solomon said at an Economic Club of New York event. Solomon said his recent meeting with New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani was productive.

items.title: Mathematicians say 'don't believe hype' on AI capabilities
items.summary: Their intervention follows claims of increasing capability from AI firms, including performance in elite international competitions and alleged solutions to thorny open questions in the field. AI "opens new and exciting opportunities, but it also raises questions that cannot be left unexamined," International Mathematical Union (IMU) vice-president Ulrike Tillmann wrote in an endorsement. AI developers face "a strong commercial incentive... to overstate the capabilities of their products," the declaration read.

items.title: Dollar in tight range as traders eye Middle East peace talks
items.summary: An agreement between the US and Iran to open the crucial strait would ease pressure on currencies of oil-importing countries such as Japan and euro zone nations, while reducing safe-haven demand for the dollar. US President Donald Trump said on Monday that talks with Iran were ongoing. The euro fell 0.03 per cent to US$1.1629.

items.title: Wall Street ends modestly higher as AI zeal overcomes Middle East jitters
items.summary: The Nasdaq ended the session nominally higher. The Philadelphia SE Semiconductor Index advanced 5.9 per cent. The Software & Services Index, battered in recent months over worries of AI disruption, ended off 3.3 per cent.

items.title: South Koreans vote in local elections seen as gauge of President Lee's first year
items.summary: SEOUL: South Koreans vote on Wednesday in local elections expected to boost President Lee Jae Myung's ruling Democratic Party, in the first nationwide ballot since his snap presidential election victory last year. "The question is not whether the Democratic Party wins many places, but how much it wins by," Heo Jin-jae, director of public opinion at Gallup Korea, told a media briefing last week. "If the Democratic Party wins Seoul and Busan, they can say they won a landslide.

items.title: Penang police bust three international scam syndicates, arrest 32
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: Police crippled three international scam syndicates operating call centres in Penang, arresting 32 individuals in separate raids around Bukit Mertajam on Monday. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the operations, conducted by the state Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID), targeted a love scam syndicate, a cryptocurrency exchange fraud syndicate involving Tether (USDT) and a phone scam syndicate impersonating Japanese police officers. He said 10 foreigners, comprising eight Chinese men, one Chinese woman and one Taiwanese man, aged between 24 and 41, were detained.

items.title: Protesting teachers in Mexico topple player statues days before World Cup
items.summary: MEXICO CITY: Protesting teachers toppled towering statues of football players on Mexico City's main promenade Tuesday and threatened growing protests before the World Cup if the government does not respond to their labour demands. A dissident wing of the national teachers' union, CNTE, called the protest and is also threatening mass demonstrations at the opening of the World Cup on June 11. Five-metre-high plastic statues of football players from around the world are located on the city's tree-shaded Paseo de la Reforma avenue, which is lined by skyscrapers.

items.title: Seven parties, one community: Why the Indian Malaysian vote could swing or stay home in GE16
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Ethnic Indian voters, long seen as a crucial swing bloc in Malaysia’s tightest electoral contests, could play a muted but potentially decisive role in the 16th general election amid political fragmentation and growing voter frustration. Numbering around 2.2 million people or 6.5 per cent of Malaysia’s population of 34.2 million, Indian voters are being courted by at least seven race-based political parties competing for influence over the community. Several smaller parties, including Makkal Sakti, the Indian Progressive Front (IPF) and the Malaysian Indian United Party (MIUP), operate under the “Friends of BN” banner, though they are not formal coalition members.

items.title: Serian Bidayuh community keeps Gawai Dayak parade alive for 56 years as three generations take part in Kampung Taee celebration
items.summary: SERIAN, June 2 — Beneath the vibrant costumes, thunder of gongs and pulse of ethnic music at today’s Gawai Dayak Parade in Kampung Taee lies a remarkable story of 56 years of unwavering commitment by the local community to preserve a tradition, passed down uninterrupted since 1970. Having first taken part at the age of ten, Malvern has never missed the opportunity to join the procession, which stands as the centrepiece of the Gawai Dayak celebration in his home village. — Bernama pic The father of one said Kampung Taee welcomes visitors every year from various states and countries, all eager to witness firsthand how the Bidayuh Bukar community in Serian celebrates Gawai Dayak.

items.title: Penang cops hunt alleged cable thieves after video showing motorcyclists chasing suspects shared online
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 2 — Police are actively tracking down suspects believed to be involved in a telecommunications cable theft incident at Jalan Batu Uban here, after a video of the incident went viral on social media today. Northeast District Deputy Police Chief Superintendent Lee Swee Sake said that his department received a report regarding the incident from Telekom Malaysia (TM) at 10.50am this morning. “The complainant, a 41-year-old TM technician, informed that he saw a viral video lasting one minute and 24 seconds at around 7am which depicted a cable theft incident by the roadside at Jalan Batu Uban 1, Gelugor.

items.title: Azalina urges Johor BN to field young, new faces to pull in youth votes
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, June 2 — Barisan Nasional (BN) component parties should consider fielding young and new faces as candidates in the upcoming Johor state election, particularly in constituencies with a high percentage of young voters, Umno Information Chief Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said. The move would not only have a positive impact on BN but would also create opportunities for a new generation of leaders to emerge as the party’s future leadership through the state legislative assembly, she added. On the dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly, Azalina said the state government under Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi had the right to dissolve the assembly with the consent of the Johor Palace.

items.title: Bumiputeras must move up value chain to become industry leaders, says DPM Zahid
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 2 — Bumiputera ownership should not be confined to small-scale businesses but must progress towards controlling larger segments of the value chain, says Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. Ahmad Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, said Bumiputera entrepreneurs should no longer be viewed merely as small vendors, ancillary suppliers or players at the end of the industrial chain, but instead be elevated to become principal manufacturers, technology owners and market leaders. We must reduce dependence on imports and build Bumiputera capabilities in high-value halal sectors,” he said when delivering the keynote address at the 2026 Bumiputera Entrepreneurs Economic Convention (KEUB) at the Terengganu Equestrian Resort here today.

items.title: Zahid says Johor BN candidate list under review, Onn Hafiz remains ‘poster boy’
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 2 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has submitted the coalition’s proposed candidates for the upcoming state election, with the list now being assessed, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today. Ahmad Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, confirmed that Onn Hafiz would remain BN’s poster boy for the election. “The Johor government is a BN government, not a Unity Government.

items.title: One-month-old baby suffers head injuries after falling out of car in Melaka crash
items.summary: MELAKA, June 2 — A one-month-old baby boy was injured after being thrown from a car in a crash involving two vehicles along the Lebuh Alor Gajah-Melaka Tengah-Jasin (AMJ) near a petrol station in Balai Panjang here last night. Acting Melaka Tengah district police chief Supt Halim Abas said the incident occurred at about 10pm when a Proton Saga with the baby boy and his parents on board was exiting a petrol station onto Lebuh AMJ before it was involved in a crash with another vehicle. “As a result of the incident, the baby, who was in his mother’s lap in the rear passenger seat, was thrown from the car onto a nearby grass verge.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms, heavy rain in seven states until 7pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds expected to hit several areas across seven states until 7pm today. The weather is also forecast for Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Yan, Pendang, Kuala Muda, and Sik in Kedah; as well as Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, and Kampar in Perak. In Terengganu, the affected areas comprise Setiu, Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu, Marang, Dungun, and Kemaman.

items.title: Ampang Line resumes phased service from June 3 after Chan Sow Lin repairs
items.summary: Ampang Line resumes phased operations from 3 June after major repair works at Chan Sow Lin Station, with full service restoration still ongoing. Passengers between Ampang and Sentul Timur will need a single transfer at Chan Sow Lin, adding about five minutes to journey times. However, passengers travelling between Ampang and Sentul Timur in both directions will need to make a single transfer at Chan Sow Lin Station.

items.title: Pahang police probe alleged senator slap involving Nik Aziz’s grandson at Janda Baik programme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — The investigation into a case involving the grandson of the late Kelantan menteri besar Tan Sri Nik Abdul Aziz Nik Mat, who allegedly claimed he was slapped by a senator during an official university programme in Janda Baik, has been handed over to Pahang police. According to Harian Metro , Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said police in Kelantan only received the report lodged by the victim at the Kota Bharu district police headquarters. Earlier, Nik Alif Aiman Abdul Ariffahmi, the grandson of the former Kelantan menteri besar, alleged that he was slapped by a senator during a university programme in Janda Baik, Pahang, on May 20.

items.title: Penang to review fire safety compliance for old, heritage buildings after Odeon blaze kills worker
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 2 — Penang will review possible improvement measures, including strengthening compliance with fire safety requirements for old and heritage buildings, following the fire at the Odeon building on Jalan Penang here, which claimed the life of a Myanmar construction worker last Saturday. “The state government views the fire incident seriously, and among the areas that could be given attention are strengthening compliance with fire safety requirements, monitoring high-risk buildings, regulating renovation works, and increasing awareness among building owners and premises operators. H’ng said existing measures include monitoring building usage, regulating renovation works, ensuring compliance with licensing requirements and coordinating with technical agencies to strengthen fire safety standards.

items.title: Johor assembly dissolution right move at right time, says Azalina
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — The dissolution of the Johor state legislative assembly is timely as the state government is currently in its strongest position, backed by positive public support for the current leadership, says Umno Information chief Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said. Online, Azalina, said the announcement by Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi falls within the state government’s rights and discretion. We even said Melaka should come first before Johor, but now Johor is first,” she said.

items.title: Official dissolution notice delivered to EC, Johor state election dates to be fixed within 60 days
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 2 — Official notification of the dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly (DUN) was submitted to the Election Commission (EC) today, confirmed Johor State Assembly Speaker Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi. The notice was submitted to formally inform the EC of the dissolution of the Johor State Assembly, which received the consent of Johor Regent Tunku Mahkota Ismail on June 1. Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the dissolution of the state assembly yesterday after receiving the consent of the Johor Regent.

items.title: Fisherman feared drowned while attempting to swim across Pahang river in Temerloh
items.summary: The victim, Samsul Azmi Ishury, 41, was reported missing at about 7.30am, prompting a search and rescue (SAR) operation by the Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM), according to an Utusan Malaysi a report today. Temerloh Fire and Rescue Station operations commander PBK II Muhammad Lutfi Burn Abdullah said his team received an emergency call at 7.30am before deploying firefighters to the scene. Rescue operations are ongoing.

items.title: Simpang Renggam tragedy: Transport minister demands swift investigation and prosecution over reckless driving deaths
items.summary: Appropriate and firm prosecution action must be taken against those responsible,” he said. Loke also stressed that road safety is a shared responsibility among all road users, warning that a driving licence is not a licence to drive recklessly. “Road safety is a shared responsibility among all road users.

items.title: Man found dead in locked vacant Ipoh building after firefighters cut grille to enter
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — A man was found dead inside a vacant two-storey building on Jalan Dato Onn Jaafar in Ipoh, Perak, on Tuesday. He said a team from the Ipoh Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the scene six minutes later and found the building’s entrance secured with a locked metal grille. Sabarodzi said that upon entering the premises, they found a man on the first floor.

items.title: Johor BN convention likely rescheduled earlier as coalition gears up to contest all 56 seats
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 2 — The Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) Convention, scheduled for June 14, may be brought forward, according to its secretary Datuk Abdul Halim Suleiman. He said the possibility arises from recent political developments in the state, particularly following the dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly yesterday. BN will contest all 56 state seats in the 16th Johor state election.

items.title: Johor election: Muda to defend Puteri Wangsa seat, field more young candidates
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Muda will defend the Puteri Wangsa state seat held by its president, Amira Aisya Abdul Aziz, in the upcoming Johor state election. Muda added that the 16th Johor state election is not merely about politics or power, but about the future direction of the state and the next generation. The Johor state assembly was dissolved yesterday, paving the way for the state election.

items.title: Police say Chinese national masterminded KL hotel party linked to drug use, supply chain probe
items.summary: Bukit Aman Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said investigations found that the event was promoted on Instagram. He said initial investigations showed that the drugs seized were for personal use and shared among party attendees, with no evidence of trafficking. Hussein said that of the 51 individuals detained in the raid, 17 were charged under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, one under Section 39A(1), and 33 under Section 12(2) of the same Act.

items.title: Storm alert: MetMalaysia warns of severe weather in northern, east coast, Sabah and Sarawak until 5pm today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning covering several states, with heavy rain, thunderstorms and strong winds expected until late this afternoon. In a Facebook post at 2pm today, MetMalaysia said the weather system is expected to affect Perlis; Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena and Padang Terap); Perak (Kuala Kangsar and Kinta); Kelantan (Jeli and Kuala Krai); Terengganu (Marang, Dungun and Kemaman); and Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Jerantut, Bentong and Kuantan). It also said similar conditions are expected in Sarawak, covering Sibu (Selangau), Bintulu, Miri (Subis, Beluru, Miri and Marudi) and Limbang, as well as Sabah, including the Interior (Kuala Penyu, Beaufort and Tambunan), West Coast, Sandakan (Kinabatangan, Beluran and Sandakan) and Kudat.

items.title: ‘Why Tiram now?’ Marina Ibrahim’s aide challenges DAP’s election strategy ahead of Johor polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — An aide to former Skudai assemblyman Marina Ibrahim has publicly questioned DAP’s decision to reportedly field her in the Tiram state seat for the upcoming Johor election, suggesting the move may have been made hastily due to seat allocation considerations. According to The Star , Ong Huai Yi, who heads Marina’s service centre, challenged Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching’s explanation that Marina was selected because she had the strongest chance of winning in the Malay-majority constituency. In a statement today, Ong questioned what preparations the party had undertaken in Tiram before considering Marina for the seat.

items.title: After 10 days, decision on continuing search for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin due tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Authorities will decide tomorrow whether to continue or call off the search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin, who disappeared at Gunung Batu Putih on May 24. According to Berita Harian , Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the decision would be made following a coordination meeting involving agencies involved in the operation. “The meeting will discuss the current status and progress of the SAR operation, as well as follow-up actions after 10 days of search efforts,” he was quoted as saying.

items.title: Penang unlikely to hold state polls alongside GE, says CM as election timelines diverge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Penang is unlikely to hold its state election simultaneously with the next general election as the gap between the two electoral cycles has become too wide, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. According to Harian Metro , Chow said the state still has about two-and-a-half years remaining in its current term, making it difficult and unfair to dissolve the state assembly prematurely simply to align the state polls with parliamentary elections. Chow was responding to a question on whether Penang would consider holding its next state election together with the general election to reduce costs amid rising living expenses.
